Security Overview

Mnemosyne is designed with security and privacy as first-class concerns.

Security Model

Threat Model

| Threat | Mitigation | |--------|------------| | Data theft (local) | Encryption at rest | | Data theft (network) | No network exposure by default | | Prompt injection | Input validation, parameterized queries | | Memory poisoning | Importance scoring, confidence thresholds | | Backup exposure | Encrypted backups |

Security Features

  • Encryption at rest: AES-256-GCM for database files
  • No cloud required: All data stays local by default
  • Input sanitization: All user inputs are parameterized
  • Audit logging: Optional access logging
  • Backup encryption: GPG or AES-encrypted backups

Compliance

| Standard | Status | Notes | |----------|--------|-------| | GDPR | Compatible | Data stays local, no third-party sharing | | HIPAA | Compatible | Encryption + access controls | | SOC 2 | Compatible | Audit logs + backup procedures |

Embedding API Calls

By default, Mnemosyne sends text to OpenAI for embedding generation. Use local embeddings (local_embeddings=True) to prevent any data leaving your machine.

© 2026 Mnemosyne. Built by Abdias Moya.
